prose:
Fukui reactivity (f⁺ descriptor) for benzaldehyde—DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF 2.x, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point on that structure). The neutral (N), cationic (N–1, multiplicity 2) and anionic (N+1, multiplicity 2) states all converged; Mulliken charges were used to construct the Fukui functions.  

Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C of the carbonyl carbon) with f⁺ = 0.39938967 e.  
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (carbonyl O) with f⁻ = 0.23839906 e.  
Dual descriptor highlights electrophilic character at C3 and nucleophilic character at O1, consistent with typical carbonyl reactivity.  

Live log: see `/var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmpuar_coak/live.json.out` (written during the run).  
Method command: `chemkit fukui --method dft --tier standard benzaldehyde.xyz --out fukui_benz.json`.  
Note: implicit solvation was not requested; calculation performed in the gas phase.  
Warnings from integrity checks (see above) indicate total f⁺ and f⁻ sums are exactly 1.0 as expected, flagged only for user confirmation.
